summary: Tested TripleSide Studio backend (17 pytest tests) and frontend (home/songs/shop/admin login/dashboard/audio). 16/17 backend tests pass. Frontend pages render, admin login + JWT localStorage persistence works, all critical data-testids in place. One backend bug: /api/checkout/status/{session_id} returns 500 immediately after session creation (Stripe API: 'No such checkout.session').

critical.issue: Returns 500 'CheckoutError: Failed to retrieve session status: No such checkout.session: cs_test_...' immediately after a successful POST /api/checkout/session. This breaks the /payment/success polling page. Likely due to a Stripe test-key/sandbox mismatch in emergentintegrations get_checkout_status vs create_checkout_session, or expected propagation delay not handled. Reproduction: POST /api/checkout/session -> use returned session_id immediately in GET /api/checkout/status/{session_id}. Mitigation suggestion: verify STRIPE_API_KEY consistency, add retry/backoff for first poll, or wrap CheckoutError to return 202/pending instead of 500.

integration_issues.issue: Because backend /checkout/status returns 500 for newly created sessions, PaymentSuccess.jsx polling will likely show error state until Stripe propagates the session.

action_items: Fix /api/checkout/status/{session_id} 500 when Stripe session not yet retrievable: catch CheckoutError 'No such checkout.session' and return {status: 'open', payment_status: 'pending'} or HTTP 202 instead of 500 so frontend polling can continue.
Verify STRIPE_API_KEY in backend/.env is valid for both create and retrieve operations (emergentintegrations).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: logger is defined after the @app.on_event('startup') seeder uses it (lines 153-265 reference logger declared at line 532). Works due to module-load ordering at runtime, but is fragile - move logging.basicConfig and logger = logging.getLogger above startup_seed.

server.py: bcrypt is imported but ADMIN_PASSWORD seed runs unconditionally on every cold start only if admin missing - good. However bcrypt hashpw in pure-python startup blocks the event loop briefly; acceptable for one-time seed.
server.py checkout_status: lacks try/except around stripe.get_checkout_status -> any Stripe SDK exception becomes a 500; should gracefully degrade to pending.
seed_data_creation: None - relied on backend startup seeding (6 songs, 6 gear, 6 products, admin)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests are pytest in /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py. Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. The only failure is checkout status polling. Frontend data-testids exist for all key elements (login-email, login-password, login-submit, ts_token in localStorage, tab-songs/gear/products/transactions, song-row-<id>, genre-filter-*, add-item-btn, edit-/delete-<id>). Audio player play action on /songs requires hovering row to reveal play button - it's an overlay icon, not a separate data-testid like 'play-song-'.
rca of the issue: Stripe returns 'No such checkout.session' for the session_id returned by create_checkout_session almost immediately. Possible causes: (1) different Stripe accounts/keys used between create and retrieve inside emergentintegrations, (2) test mode session not yet propagated, (3) sk_test_emergent key behaves differently for retrieve. Mitigation in backend: wrap get_checkout_status in try/except and return pending status with 200 so frontend polling continues without breaking UX.
